enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request (from Chris) about Conor McGregor, offering mild approval of McGregor's flamboyance before pivoting to his core idea: a 'fight suit' that would make MMA safer and more frequent. He issues a mock callout challenge to McGregor, proposing a fight preceded by a 100-mile mountain run to test endurance, drawing on Roman history to argue that fatigue is the real determinant of battle outcomes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Mildly approves — sees a possible 'seed' of something useful in his showmanship
Considers it barbaric; fighters should use fight suits so no one gets hurt and bouts can happen daily
Claims to be the 'godfather of MMA' solely because of the fight suit concept, not fighting ability
Fighters fear losing because they fight too infrequently; more fights via suit would eliminate that fear
Mass production would make fight suits affordable like bicycles, enabling mainstream adoption
